item.title: Junior Staff Post - Noncompliant Images
item.content:encoded: The image in this article has been removed for noncompliance with the site's CC-BY-SA license.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wouldn't other humans note the horrifying appearance of SCP-3926-1?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made an account to give this one (and that cute everglades slug) a vote, gave me a laugh :)

Though I find it kind of unfortunate that you simply gloss over most of what happens to the humans. (at least, physically) they turn green and lose their faces and genitals? what? that probably deserves a little more fleshing out, or explaination, or reaction, or anything. how do they talk to do their conversion attempts? how do they continue to biologically function? are they green because they grew scales, or just now have green human skin for some reason?

its not something that needs to be super thought-out but those are questions the foundation would probably try to look at, even to just say 'unknown'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it. It's been done before, sure, but I think this has enough of its own schtick to stand out on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I've read this type of talk show anomaly multiple times now. The addendum consists almost entirely of the "ascend humanity and join us" filling you see as a feature for other articles, and I felt like nothing deeper was revealed about Tyal'k'nec.

Coupled with the random nonstandard image format and "Item Description", I'll pass on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think we have seen the changes made, and daresay that they have given this SCP its own schtick to work off on.

item.title: Improvements
item.content:encoded:
I'm liking the changes to the page. It really does feel a lot more fleshed out. Good work on the changes, only thing I'm curious about now is the mention of pods, and families. Like does this mean human kids kinda molt differently? Or that they can reproduce without the show, gaining converts? The couple thrashing in the second advert kinda implies it. XD Just the thought of that in this kinda scenic peaceful village advertisement makes me laugh. ^^;

Also any plans for any unique pets/creatures that Tyal'k'nec could be advertising for sale? Like your very own creepy leech/squid/fishdog? That Foundation personnel could also encounter living with the -1 instances. I imagine the advert going something like pictures of dogs barking or attacking humans and 3926-1. The same narrator (Possibly with his own designation) would talk about the advantages of them, over the oppressive beasts of mankind. And how they're much better suited to their new homes/settlements. Or there might be an instructional advert to convert dogs into these creatures. Maybe something like. 'Want to shed your human form? But worried about how Fido is going to fair in one of our communities? Well fear not. Tyal'k'nec's easy 3-step guide will allow your pooch to survive in your new swampy home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I enjoy the talk show transcript. It really makes your SCP seem more personable and interesting. I do think you need to rework the SCP -1 and it's second stage instances as I felt kind of confused there. Do SCP-1 instances teleport to the studio to view the show live? Or do they simply stay at home and get effected? I think if you had them in their second stage still being intelligent, and proclaiming the benefits of the show, that would be better. Perhaps you can have some incidents of Foundation Personnel shutting down radio broadcasts and Internet webpages, created by these instances to encourage people to watch?

Maybe have only 'live' shows cause the anomalous effects to humans, recorded episodes could lack the anomalous effects. Hinting that its something in particular about the live show, that affects people? Like hearing Tyal'k'nec is the trigger, but whatever that is, gets lost or scrambled in recordings? I'd also suggest changing SCP-3926-1 in stage 2, to a new designation, maybe SCP-3926-2? And lastly I found it a little strange that Amnestic's aren't used on people. Would they be effective at curing or reversing the effects of 3926? Would they work on Stage 2 instances?

I've given you a +1 vote for now. I'm looking forward to you expanding on this SCP more, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe this has honest potential to be something very intruging. As MrWrong said though, it feels kind of weak for the -1 instances to just become killing machines. Let them still have a form of sentience, perhaps they could also try to convert others by forcing them to listen to Tyal'k'nec preach (maybe by incapacitating/handicapping them). Would give it a few more layers in my opinion.

I really liked the actual show script though. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for your input. I'll flesh that out more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, while there are similarities with SCP-1171, I think there can be enough distinctions to warrant this article's existence. However, while I don't mind the effect of changing humans into -2 instances, I find it a weak point in the story for them to simply be reduced to things that kill humans.

It would be helpful if you show some depth in those -2 instances. What are their perspectives after shedding their humanity? Will they preach the words of Tyal'k'nec, as it did? Why would they want to engage in violence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the quirkiness you put into the show transcript. It reminded me of when I used to watch Americans Funniest Home Videos as a child. I feel that this could be expanded a bit though.
